title: Climate & Energy Survey — U.S. House District 4

questions.question: Global Warming
questions.statementA: Global warming, driven by human activities threatens our well-being
questions.statementB: The climate has always changed by itself, and is nothing to worry about

questions.question: Energy Portfolio Choices
questions.statementA: Continue to subsidize the already established fossil fuel industry
questions.statementB: Subsidize carbon-free solutions, including nuclear and renewable energy

questions.question: Climate and the Economy
questions.statementA: We can thrive by proactively addressing climate change
questions.statementB: Prioritizing climate change solutions negatively impacts business and economy

questions.question: Making Important Public Decisions
questions.statementA: I mostly trust my own lived experiences, judgements, beliefs and values
questions.statementB: I mostly seek out opinions and perspectives different from my own

questions.question: Power Generation on Public Lands
questions.statementA: I support allowing renewable power generation on public lands
questions.statementB: I am against allowing renewable power generation on public lands

questions.question: Repealing the EPA's Endangerment Findings
questions.statementA: Repealing is a mistake which will ultimately increase pollution and harm the climate
questions.statementB: Repealing removes unnecessary regulations and will improve the economy

questions.question: Climate Change Education Act
questions.statementA: Favorable, I would vote for the Act
questions.statementB: Unfavorable, I would vote against the Act

questions.question: Regulating CO2 and CH4
questions.statementA: I support regulating CO2 and CH4 as pollutants
questions.statementB: I oppose regulating CO2 and CH4 as pollutants

questions.question: Carbon-based Border Tariffs
questions.statementA: I support carbon-based border adjustments on imports
questions.statementB: I oppose carbon-based border tariffs on imports

questions.question: Forest Management
questions.statementA: I support management guided by climate science, e.g., using forests as carbon sinks
questions.statementB: I support business as usual with no special references to climate science

questions.question: What would you do, as a member of Congress, about climate change in terms of policy and legislation?
